summary.intent: Implement two new charts (Friction Events and Open Items per Checkpoint) on the metrics dashboard, then consolidate three scattered chart documentation files into one authoritative doc under docs/.
summary.outcome: Both charts were implemented in a new QualityDebtSection component, documentation was consolidated and later re-audited to match the actual implementation, and a bug preventing the new charts from showing data was found and fixed (summary field was in session-level metadata, not top-level).

repo: The `summary` field (friction/open_items) lives in session-level metadata.json (e.g. `01/60328b41c7/0/metadata.json`), NOT in the top-level checkpoint metadata.json.
Each section component (AttributionSection, TokenSection, etc.) duplicates a local `fmtLabel` helper rather than sharing one — established pattern in this codebase.
There is no `commit_message` field in checkpoint data; labels are date-based only.
Charts 21, 22, 23 were fully implemented despite being listed as 'proposed' in the old documentation — docs had drifted significantly from implementation.
Cache Leverage Score (Chart 10), Commit Activity Timeline (Chart 18), and Attribution Trend (Chart 20) were removed from implementation but remained in docs.
Three undocumented charts existed in code: AI Usage by Developer, Prompts per Checkpoint, and BMAD Command Frequency.

code.finding: Live fetch path hardcoded `summary: { friction: [], open_items: [] }` — the field was never populated even though topMeta was already fetched.
Top-level checkpoint metadata.json is fetched here as `topMeta` but does NOT contain summary data; summary is in session-level metadata.
`parseMetadata` correctly handles summary.friction and summary.open_items but is only used on the cached/pre-parsed path, not the live fetch path.

workflow: When adding a new data field to displayed charts, always trace the full data pipeline from fetch → parse → component to confirm the field isn't silently dropped at an intermediate step.
After significant refactoring, audit docs against implementation rather than assuming they're in sync — in this project docs had multiple phantom entries and missing entries.
Pre-existing TypeScript errors in unrelated files can obscure whether new code introduced errors; grep for the new file specifically to confirm it's clean.

friction: The fix for empty charts required two iterations: first attempt incorrectly targeted top-level checkpoint metadata, second attempt correctly identified session-level metadata as the source.
The user had to share a screenshot to confirm the first fix didn't work — no automated way to verify runtime data population from the build.
Three separate chart documentation files existed with overlapping and conflicting content, requiring a full audit pass.
open_items: The `fmtLabel` helper is duplicated across all section components — could be extracted to a shared utility.
No automated test coverage for the session-level summary parsing added to `parseSessionMeta`.
The dashboard has no integration/e2e test that verifies charts render with real data shapes.
